Class SQLite

java.lang.Object
Controller.SQLite
public class SQLite extends Object

    resetFailedAttempts(String username)
    Resets failed_attempts to 0 for the given user (called on successful login).

    Sets the locked column for a user.
    void

    Records the timestamp and status (SUCCESS / FAILED) of the last login attempt.

    Stores a BCrypt-hashed password for the given user.

    Updates product details identified by oldName.
    void
    updateUserRole(String username, int role)
    Changes a user's role.

    • DEBUG_MODE

      public int DEBUG_MODE

  • Constructor Details

    • SQLite

      public SQLite()

  • Method Details

    • createNewDatabase

      public void createNewDatabase()

    • createHistoryTable

      public void createHistoryTable()

    • createLogsTable

      public void createLogsTable()

    • createProductTable

      public void createProductTable()

    • createUserTable

      public void createUserTable()
      Creates the users table including the three new security columns.

    • migrateDatabase

      public void migrateDatabase()
      Adds new security columns to an existing users table that was created before the schema update. SQLite does not support IF NOT EXISTS in ALTER TABLE, so each attempt is wrapped in its own try-catch; a "duplicate column" error means the column already exists — safe to ignore.

    • dropHistoryTable

      public void dropHistoryTable()

    • dropLogsTable

      public void dropLogsTable()

    • dropProductTable

      public void dropProductTable()

    • dropUserTable

      public void dropUserTable()

    • addHistory

      public void addHistory(String username, String name, int stock, String timestamp)

    • addLogs

      public void addLogs(String event, String username, String desc, String timestamp)

    • addProduct

      public void addProduct(String name, int stock, double price)

    • addUser

      public void addUser(String username, String password)
      Registers a new user (role defaults to 2 / Client). Password is BCrypt-hashed.

    • addUser

      public void addUser(String username, String password, int role)
      Registers a new user with an explicit role. Password is BCrypt-hashed.

    • getHistory

      public ArrayList<History> getHistory()

    • getHistoryByUsername

      public ArrayList<History> getHistoryByUsername(String username)
      Returns purchase history for a single username only.

    • getLogs

      public ArrayList<Logs> getLogs()

    • clearLogs

      public void clearLogs()

    • getProduct

      public ArrayList<Product> getProduct()

    • getProduct

      public Product getProduct(String name)
      Returns a Product by name. Uses PreparedStatement to prevent SQL injection.

    • getUsers

      public ArrayList<User> getUsers()
      Returns all users including the new security fields.

    • getUserByUsername

      public User getUserByUsername(String username)
      Looks up a single user by username. Uses PreparedStatement — safe against SQL injection. Returns null if the user does not exist.

    • updatePassword

      public void updatePassword(String username, String hashedPassword)
      Stores a BCrypt-hashed password for the given user.

    • incrementFailedAttempts

      public void incrementFailedAttempts(String username)
      Increments failed_attempts by 1 for the given user.

    • resetFailedAttempts

      public void resetFailedAttempts(String username)
      Resets failed_attempts to 0 for the given user (called on successful login).

    • disableUser

      public void disableUser(String username)
      Sets role=1 (Disabled) for the given user — used for automatic login lockout.

    • updateLastLogin

      public void updateLastLogin(String username, String timestamp, String status)
      Records the timestamp and status (SUCCESS / FAILED) of the last login attempt.

    • updateUserRole

      public void updateUserRole(String username, int role)
      Changes a user's role. Used by admin EDIT ROLE action.

    • updateProduct

      public void updateProduct(String oldName, String newName, int stock, double price)
      Updates product details identified by oldName.

    • purchaseProduct

      public boolean purchaseProduct(String name, int quantity)
      Attempts to purchase product quantity atomically. Returns true on success, false if product not found or insufficient stock.

    • setUserLocked

      public void setUserLocked(String username, int locked)
      Sets the locked column for a user. Pass 1 to lock, 0 to unlock. This is the manual admin lock — separate from the automatic role=1 disable.

    • removeUser

      public void removeUser(String username)

    • removeProduct

      public void removeProduct(String name)
